title: papaver alpinum subsp. kerneri
intro: Papaver alpinum subsp. kerneri, also known as Kern's Alpine Poppy, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the poppy family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties due to its content of alkaloids, particularly protopine and allocryptopine. The medicinal properties of Papaver alpinum subsp. kerneri have been used to treat various health issues, including pain, inflammation, and respiratory problems, offering benefits such as reduced swelling and improved breathing. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of tinctures, infusions, and extracts, which are often used in naturopathic and homeopathic medicine.
